judgment.summary: Workday exhibits strong growth and cash flow generation, but its valuation metrics present a mixed picture with high trailing P/E and a significant forward P/E discount.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last reported quarter (ending 2026-01-31) saw EPS of $2.47, beating the estimate of $2.32 by 6.4%. Next earnings date is 2026-05-21.
positives: Strong earnings growth of 62.9% year-over-year and 54.3% in the last reported quarter.
Robust free cash flow of $3.01B on a trailing twelve-month basis.
Gross margin of 75.7% indicates strong pricing power and efficient cost of goods sold.
PEG ratio of 0.50 suggests the stock may be undervalued relative to its earnings growth.
Analyst consensus is a strong buy with a mean target price of $179.22, implying significant upside from the current spot price.
negatives: Trailing P/E of 48.88 is high, indicating a premium valuation based on past performance.
Forward P/E of 10.17 is significantly lower than trailing P/E, suggesting a large expected drop in earnings or a very optimistic growth outlook.
Operating margin of 12.0% and profit margin of 7.3% are modest for a software company.
Return on equity of 8.2% and return on assets of 3.6% are relatively low.
Debt/Equity ratio of 48.96, while not excessive, indicates a notable level of leverage.

news.paragraph: Workday (WDAY) is facing mixed news as recent headlines highlight both positive developments and concerns. While some reports suggest a significant stock surge and continued expansion plans with AI investments, others point to a downgrade from BWG Global and commentary from Jim Cramer suggesting AI displacement risks. Cantor Fitzgerald adjusted its price target to $160 while maintaining an Overweight rating, indicating a potentially bullish outlook despite some headwinds.

headlines.title: Jim Cramer Says Workday Has Become a “Poster Boy” For AI Displacement
